items.title: Mary has fallen into an aged care 'black hole' and sees no way out

children.content: After being stuck in a hospital in Canberra for 19 months, paraplegic 84-year-old Mary Wansey has lost hope of ever finding a place in aged care.

gallery.altText: A woman with grey hair and glasses sits in a wheelchair in a hospital room.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with grey hair and glasses sits in a wheelchair in a hospital room.

items.title: Too young for social media, but old enough to be locked up

children.content: As a nation, we have decided that a teenager cannot navigate a social media algorithm, but a 10-year-old can navigate a courtroom. For Australia's 2025 UN youth representative Satara Uthayakumaran, the contradiction is impossible to ignore.

image.altText: A young women sits listening to young unidentified detainees in a youth detention centre

items.title: Motorcyclist dies in crash in southern NSW

children.content: The state has reported its first road death of the Easter long weekend, with the detour expected to add five hours' travel time for some motorists.

image.altText: Generic sign of the Monaro Highway at Nimmitabel.

items.title: Thousands flock to National Folk Festival despite fuel cost concerns

children.content: The lure of a lively celebration of folk art outweighs concerns about the cost of fuel for thousands travelling to Canberra for the 60th National Folk Festival.

thumbnail.altText: A man dressed in an elaborate costume.

items.title: Raiders' season lurches towards disaster as Knights run riot

children.content: The reigning minor premiers are in freefall after Canberra slumped to a fourth-straight loss at the hands of a resurgent Knights outfit.

image.altText: A group of rugby league players in orange celebrate while players in green watch on

items.title: 'Cameras watching': Horrifying trend of technology-based abuse

children.content: One in three women on temporary visas are experiencing domestic and family violence, safety advocates say.

image.altText: A professional with glasses and a sombre expression.

items.title: The 'quiet details' shared by two artists working 50 years apart

children.content: The new exhibition at the National Portrait Gallery brings together the work of two Australian artists working nearly half a century apart, and asks visitors to find the sparks connecting them.

Christina Koch silhouette looks back at Earth from Artemis

articles.title: People have travelled further into space than ever before — see what that journey is like for yourself

children.content: If all goes well for NASA's mission, the astronauts aboard could fly the furthest that humans have ever gone. What should we be looking out for?

image.altText: A 3D wireframe rocket with 3 male and 1 female astronauts pose with looming large moon behind

children.content: The Artemis II astronauts have travelled further from Earth than any humans before. The crew is now on a historic six-hour flyby of the Moon, capturing never-before-seen views of its far side. Follow live.

items.title: 'We are looking back at you': Mission communication restored with Artemis II

updates.title: Earthshine and solar corona seen during eclipse
It's homeward bound for Artemis II after the Moon flyby
A solar eclipse for the finale

items.title: Pro-climate son-in-law to Rupert Murdoch backs Farrer independent

children.content: Backers of Farrer independent candidate Michelle Milthorpe are celebrating after securing the support of a prominent pro-climate farmer who is also the son-in-law of media mogul Rupert Murdoch.

image.altText: Michelle Milthorpe talks to two people with their backs to the camera in the bistro of a pub.

items.title: Fuel thieves targeting NSW truckers as they sleep

children.content: The owner of a trucking company in Western Sydney says most cases of fuel theft have occurred when his drivers have parked on the side of the road for the night.

image.altText: Hand holding petrol cap with cap inside tank

items.title: Government focused on supply, 'not considering' fuel rationing: Rowland

children.content: Attorney-General Michelle Rowland says the government is focused on securing supply as she insists fuel rationing is not being considered right now. Follow live.

image.altText: Michelle Rowland press conference

items.title: A luxury beef giant's cattle died of thirst. Regulators were told later

children.content: The lack of investigation into deaths at the $700 million beef business has been described as "completely unacceptable" by a legal expert who argued industry had captured government regulators.

image.altText: A herd of AACo's wagyu heifers at Westholme station in Queensland.

items.title: Zein speaks broken Arabic — here's how she made peace with that

children.content: For Heywire winner Zein, her family is remembered in the fragments she remembers of Syria.

items.title: Israeli settler violence in the West Bank deepens against backdrop of Iran war

children.content: Under the cover of regional conflict, Israelis who have established illegal settlements and outposts in the West Bank become emboldened to take over more land as world attention remains on the war in Iran.

image.altText: Mourners sit outside a hospital morgue in the West Bank.

items.title: How China uses ferries and fishing boats to pressure Taiwan

children.content: China has quietly mobilised thousands of fishing boats twice in recent months to form floating barriers at least 300 kilometres long.

image.altText: A graphic features fishing vessels, a target and maps featuring the flags of China and Taiwan.

items.title: The first world country in the grip of a poverty crisis

children.content: Once a mighty empire, Britain is now a broken kingdom. A staggering one fifth of the nation lives in poverty and that number is rising.

items.title: Hidden costs mean many comedians make 'zero profit' from festival runs

children.content: Doing a run of shows at the Melbourne International Comedy Festival is the pinnacle for a lot of Australian comedians, but the festival's tens of thousands of punters are rarely privy to the behind-the-scenes costs.

items.title: ASX surges 2pc after modest gains on Wall St as Iran deadline looms

children.content: The Australian surges out of the gates after a four-day long weekend, as traders appear to look through the ongoing uncertainty in the Middle East. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: Giant screen on the wall of the ASX, with large green numbers, showing the price of shares which have risen.

items.title: Man sent home from hospital with '10/10' pain ends up losing chunk of leg

children.content: An Australian Navy candidate says he nearly lost his leg following delays in diagnosis and treatment of his emergency condition by two Perth hospitals.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a man's leg with a large scar running down his shin and a brace hanging down the side as he sits on a bench.

items.title: 'Are you sure?': First Nations voters asked about identity at polling booths

children.content: South Australia's Aboriginal Affairs Minister will consider an independent review of the state's Voice to Parliament
election, following claims some voters were queried about their racial background at polling booths, while others were forced to queue for hours.

items.title: 'No one else should go through this': Young job hunters a target in scam surge

children.content: Job seekers should be wary of sharing their bank account information with potential employers, as job scams targeting young people surge, according to Scamwatch.

items.title: Middle East war causes PVC prices to skyrocket

children.content: Conflict in the Middle East has disrupted petroleum and gas supply chains, causing PVC prices to skyrocket in Australia.

items.title: Residents worry link between minister and wind farm project could dilute advocacy

children.content: Despite the links being declared, some locals worry politicians with a financial stake in a planned development in Tasmania's north will have little sympathy for their concerns about the project.

items.title: Aussie stars testing the waters at Australian Open

children.content: Molly O'Callaghan and Kyle Chalmers claimed Australian Open gold medals in the 100m freestyle events, while Sam Short showed his 400m rivals a clean pair of heels on night one at the Gold Coast Aquatic Centre.

items.title: 'I hate points': Gunston's last-second behind helps Hawthorn claim thriller

children.content: Hawthorn's Jack Gunston kicks a behind in the dying seconds to defeat Geelong by one point in an Easter Monday classic.

items.title: Marshall questions penalty against Tigers despite Eels star's ugly ankle injury

children.content: As the Eels grapple with another potentially season-ending injury to Bailey Simonsson, Wests Tigers coach Benji Marshall says he is not even sure the tackle that led to it was worth a penalty.

items.title: The unexpected place new collectors are finding affordable art

children.content: Horse drawings and handcrafted ceramics from local artists and artisans are among the arts and crafts you can find at the Sydney Royal Easter Show.
